subtitle: The hardware and software I actually reach for, from the optics bench to this site.

sections.heading: Research & lab
sections.body: Optical modeling happens in **COMSOL Multiphysics** — finite-element simulations of light propagation, waveguides, and photoacoustic wave fields — and in **Zemax OpticStudio** when I need ray tracing and tolerancing across a whole system. Most of my inverse-problem, signal-processing, and Jones-matrix polarization code is still **MATLAB**. Optomechanical parts get drawn in **SolidWorks** before they ever hit the bench.

sections.heading: Computation & ML
sections.body: **Python** does almost everything else. Differentiable forward models and physics-informed networks lean on **PyTorch** and **JAX**; the ordinary numerics fall to **NumPy**, **SciPy**, and **scikit-learn**. Exploratory work lives in **Jupyter**, and manuscripts in **LaTeX**.

sections.heading: Development
sections.body: I write in **VS Code** with Vim keybindings and keep everything in **Git** and **GitHub**. This site runs on **Astro**, **Tailwind CSS**, and **TypeScript**, deployed to **Netlify** — the [colophon](/colophon/) has the full build. I use **Claude Code** as a pair-programmer on it.

sections.heading: Writing & notes
sections.body: **Obsidian** holds my reading notes and the rougher drafts that eventually turn into the [notes](/notes/) on this site.

sections.heading: Hardware
sections.body: A workstation with an NVIDIA GPU for COMSOL runs and model training. In the lab that means swept-source lasers, fiber test benches, optical spectrum analyzers, and the optomechanics that go with them.
